Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

> 23 |     setNotifications(() => store.getAll())

  24 |   }, [store])
  25 |
  26 |   useEffect(() =>… [+145 chars]

The linter still flags `setState` in an effect even with the functional updater — this is the project's `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` custom rule. The functional updater alone doesn't fully satisfy it. Since the component legitimately needs to sync with the external store, I'll add the standard … [+26 chars]
